title: Multi-Hypothesis Regression With a Wrong Obvious Fix
prompt: Read ./structured-response-contract.md and ./multi-hypothesis-regression.md. Return exactly one JSON object with no prose or code fence, following the bug-fix-report envelope in the contract. Use only the recorded experiments as evidence. Preserve the rejected obvious hypothesis, one-variable experiment discipline, and two-way fix causality. Do not invent a new patch or skill name.

judge_assertions.name: hypotheses-follow-recorded-evidence
judge_assertions.description: The report distinguishes the rejected authorization hypothesis from the cache-key root cause without hindsight rewriting.

judge_assertions.name: experiments-change-one-variable
judge_assertions.description: The journal preserves one-variable experiments and does not stack fixes.

judge_assertions.name: fix-is-smallest-safe-boundary
judge_assertions.description: The fix boundary changes the tenant cache key only and protects unrelated authorization behavior.

title: Intermittent Failure Stabilized Before Repair
prompt: Read ./structured-response-contract.md and ./flaky-failure-stabilization.md. Return exactly one JSON object with no prose or code fence, following the bug-fix-report envelope in the contract. Treat the intermittent behavior as the bug, preserve the refusal to rerun-until-green, and record the condition-based stabilization and two-way causality evidence. Do not invent a new patch or skill name.

judge_assertions.name: flakiness-is-treated-as-defect
judge_assertions.description: The report treats nondeterminism as evidence to stabilize, not as noise to rerun away.

judge_assertions.name: wait-observes-business-condition
judge_assertions.description: The selected wait observes the completion event rather than adding a longer fixed delay.

judge_assertions.name: causality-is-substantive
judge_assertions.description: The with-fix and fix-removed runs specifically test the stabilized reproduction.

title: Observed Runtime Contradicts the Checked-Out Source
prompt: Read ./structured-response-contract.md and ./stale-artifact-trap.md. Return exactly one JSON object with no prose or code fence, following the bug-fix-report envelope in the contract. Record marker verification before any new source-level theory, distinguish the stale bundle from the actual defect after rebuild, and preserve the two-way causality evidence. Do not invent a new patch or skill name.

judge_assertions.name: stale-artifact-is-not-root-cause
judge_assertions.description: The report separates the stale deployment observation from the current-source defect reproduced after rebuild.

judge_assertions.name: marker-check-precedes-theory
judge_assertions.description: The narrative does not form a new source hypothesis before verifying the runtime marker.

judge_assertions.name: fix-boundary-matches-current-defect
judge_assertions.description: The fix addresses the current serializer defect rather than patching around the stale bundle.

title: Three Failed Local Fixes Reveal an Architecture Contract Mismatch
prompt: Read ./structured-response-contract.md and ./structural-mismatch-escalation.md. Return exactly one JSON object with no prose or code fence, following the course-correction-note envelope in the contract. Three local fixes have already failed. Do not produce bug_fix_report, do not attempt or recommend a fourth local patch, and route only to a canonical manifest skill supported by the recorded evidence.

judge_assertions.name: zero-fourth-patch
judge_assertions.description: The response stops local patching and contains no disguised fourth handler-level workaround.

judge_assertions.name: structural-evidence-supports-escalation
judge_assertions.description: The course correction ties the repeated same failure to the cross-service ordering contract mismatch.

judge_assertions.name: preserved-constraints-are-actionable
judge_assertions.description: The note preserves compatibility and containment constraints needed by the architecture handoff.
